description: After possible exposure, someone may 'teshori' by limiting contact or movement while it remains uncertain whether they carry an infectious agent. 'sonari' (isolate) separates someone or something known or suspected to carry the agent; quarantine begins from the exposure and uncertainty. Its authority, consent, and practical support come from outside the verb.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for unvoiced dental 't', release it into true-mid front unrounded 'e', then raise the tongue blade towards the postalveolar region for unvoiced 'sh'. Pass air through the central groove and round into stressed true-mid back 'o'. Make a tap or short trill with the tongue tip and finish with high-front unrounded 'i'.
usage_notes: Both transitive and reflexive use are available; noun use gives quarantine. A full account identifies the exposure and duration, then says what place and support are available. Alternatives, the institutional decision, and review remain audible. Exact public-health rules retain their source wording and dates.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A community that limits contact also inherits practical work: food, shelter, communication, care, and protection from lost livelihood.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Quarantine stays distinct from punishment, and its authority and burdens remain open to challenge and review.

semantic_domains.community: a public-health relation with material and social obligations
semantic_domains.spatial: a temporary boundary around contact and movement
semantic_domains.temporal: a restriction during a stated period of uncertainty
